mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, directly compressing valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, which compresses corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: The hypothesis fails if 5-day forward returns following a 0.2pp UNRATE jump are not statistically negative (mean return > -0.5% with p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between housing starts and revolving consumer credit indicates a structural shift in household balance sheets. When housing construction slows while credit expansion accelerates, equity markets price in a near-term consumption boom, yielding positive forward returns.
failure_condition: The signal loses predictive power if the cross-sectional correlation between HOUST and RSAFS exceeds 0.6, rendering the divergence metric noise.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained industrial production growth above trend reflects robust manufacturing capacity utilization and healthy order backlogs. This supply-side strength supports corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if 60-day forward returns show no positive correlation with INDPRO growth rates exceeding 0.5% month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth directly expands corporate revenue bases and improves earnings per share trajectories. This fundamental demand surge translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week trading window as analysts revise forecasts upward.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if quarterly GDP revisions do not lead to positive 5-day forward returns in the subsequent month.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0%, borrowing costs suppress equity valuations and reduce leverage capacity for corporate buybacks. This monetary tightening regime forces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
failure_condition: The hypothesis fails if 20-day forward returns remain positive or neutral when FEDFUNDS stays above 5.0% for consecutive quarters.

mechanism_narrative: The joint behavior of nonfarm payrolls and the unemployment rate determines the labor market tightness premium. When payroll growth exceeds 150,000 while unemployment remains below 5.0%, wage pressures compress margins and drive negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction signal fails if the combined PAYEMS/UNRATE metric shows no statistically significant negative correlation with 60-day forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer revolving credit balances signal increased household leverage and near-term consumption strength. This demand impulse boosts retail and service sector earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns within a weekly horizon.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if 5-day forward returns do not positively correlate with month-over-month RSAFS growth exceeding 1.5%.
